title: Replicate the log, derive the state
description: Agree on an ordered log of commands via consensus and apply it deterministically on every replica, rather than shipping mutated state between nodes.
body: Use when building any replicated service that must stay consistent across nodes. Do not ship snapshots of mutated in-memory state and hope they converge, and do not apply commands in different orders on different replicas. Actions: define a deterministic state machine, push every mutation as an ordered log entry through consensus, commit only after a quorum acknowledges, and apply entries in log order on each node. Verify replicas converge by comparing the applied state at a committed index. Validation signals include identical state-machine output across nodes at the same commit index and a Jepsen history with no divergence. Risk boundary: non-deterministic apply logic (map iteration order, local time, RNG) silently diverges replicas; treat any non-determinism in the apply path as a blocking defect and escalate before deploy.
